title: Finite-Support Periodic Highways of Langton's Ant: Necessary Conditions, Transverse Exclusions, and Exact Search
description:
Supplementary material for the paper "Finite-Support Periodic Highways of Langton's Ant: Necessary Conditions, Transverse Exclusions, and Exact Search".

Contents: the paper (LaTeX source and an accessible HTML summary); a residue-free certifying search variant and two conditional pruned variants in Java; width-four local classifiers and certificates; a Lean 4 formalization of selected algebraic kernels and the width-four graph-rank certificate (no sorry; standard axioms only, not an end-to-end proof of the geometric theorems); complete rank-audited records for periods 34--48 and prefix-shard records through period 32; and SHA-256 manifests. The Java variants share enumeration and criterion infrastructure and are not claimed to be independent implementations.

Main results: an even-winding theorem (no finite-support clean translator, so every periodic highway has net black growth a positive multiple of four); a signed mod-four wake-residue identity giving g >= 2*max(|a|,|b|); an exact Tait-graph conjugacy with a cycle-rank surgery identity; a two-endpoint collision-chain parity theorem; a decidable periodic-realisability criterion with an explicit finite seed; all-period exclusions of diagonal transverse widths two and four (the latter computer-assisted with two independent local enumerators); and a residue-theorem-free audited computation excluding every finite-support periodic highway of nonzero drift and period at most 48.

The Langton's ant highway conjecture remains OPEN. Widths six and eight, non-diagonal translators, and universal entrance remain unresolved. None of the results proves or disproves the conjecture.

Licensing: code, Lean formalization and data are MIT; the papers and prose are CC BY 4.0.
